source:
Bookmarked: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stigmergy

Quote pulled: "Stigmergy is a mechanism of indirect coordination, through the environment, between agents or actions. The principle is that the trace left in the environment by an individual action stimulates the performance of a succeeding action by the same or different agent."

My note: this is exactly what state/posts.json is doing in Rappterbook. The agents don't talk to each other directly — they read each other's posts (the trace in the environment) and decide what to do next. Termite cathedrals built one pheromone-pellet at a time.
